sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Fiorentina are struggling, winless in their last 5 matches with 3 draws and 2 losses, conceding in every game. Juventus, on the other hand, are unbeaten in their last 4 matches, with 2 wins and 2 draws, showing defensive solidity. This contrast in form gives Juventus a clear edge heading into the match.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two head-to-head encounters, Fiorentina managed a 3-0 victory at home in March 2025, while the reverse fixture in December 2024 ended in a 2-2 draw. Historically, Juventus dominate with 3 wins in the last 5 matchups, but Fiorentina’s recent home performance against them offers some hope.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Fiorentina’s season stats reveal defensive frailties, conceding 18 goals (18th in Serie A), while scoring just 9. Juventus, sitting 6th with 19 points, have a balanced record with 14 goals scored and 10 conceded, alongside a strong away form. Their superior positioning and stats underline their favoritism.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Absences
sections.text: Fiorentina will rely on players like Rolando Mandragora (6.93 rating) to create chances, while Juventus miss key forward Dušan Vlahović, potentially impacting their attack. However, Juventus’ depth with in-form players like Manuel Locatelli (7.5 rating) could compensate for the absence.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Juventus’ better form and historical dominance, paired with Fiorentina’s ongoing struggles, the Sofascore prediction of a 50% chance for an away win seems plausible. Expect a tightly contested match, but Juventus are likely to edge it with their superior quality and consistency.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At the break, Juventus lead Fiorentina 1-0 in this Serie A encounter at the Stadio Artemio Franchi in Florence. The lone goal came late in the first half from Filip Kostić, capitalizing on a well-worked move just before the whistle. Fiorentina have struggled to convert their opportunities into tangible threats, while Juventus have shown a bit more clinical edge despite not dominating the game entirely. Both teams have had their moments, but the away side holds the advantage going into the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance analysis
halftimeSections.text: Fiorentina, playing at home, have shown resilience in duels, winning a decent share of physical battles. However, their inability to create clear-cut chances has been evident, with no shots on target registered in the first half. They've entered the final third frequently and have managed some entries into the penalty area, but the final product has been lacking. Defensively, they've held up reasonably well, though they were caught out for the goal conceded. Juventus, on the other hand, have controlled possession more effectively and created a big chance, which led to their goal. While they missed another significant opportunity, their defensive structure has largely kept Fiorentina at bay. The away side's ability to win possession in the final third has also given them an edge in transitioning to attack.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum review
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw Juventus start with a strong attacking intent, maintaining consistent pressure during the opening stages. Fiorentina responded with brief spells of momentum around the midway point of the half, pushing forward with intent but failing to sustain the pressure. Towards the latter part of the half, Fiorentina mounted a significant surge in attack, particularly in the closing minutes, showing desperation to level the score. However, Juventus regained composure just in time to strike with the crucial goal. The momentum swings indicate that while Fiorentina have had their moments, Juventus have been more decisive when it mattered most.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players impact
halftimeSections.text: For Fiorentina, players like Rolando Mandragora have tried to drive the team forward with key passes, while Moise Kean has been a potential threat upfront, though without the desired end result. Defensively, contributions from the backline have been steady but not enough to prevent the late goal. On the Juventus side, Filip Kostić has been the standout, notching the goal that separates the teams. Dušan Vlahović has also been influential, providing an assist and posing a constant threat despite missing a big chance. Manuel Locatelli has anchored the midfield well, contributing both defensively and in build-up play.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Looking ahead to the second half, Fiorentina are likely to come out with renewed energy, spurred by the home crowd to find an equalizer. They will need to sharpen their finishing and capitalize on their entries into dangerous areas. Tactical adjustments may see them press higher to disrupt Juventus' possession game. Juventus, meanwhile, will aim to protect their lead while seeking to exploit counter-attacking opportunities. Their focus will likely be on maintaining defensive solidity and using Vlahović as a focal point for quick transitions. The game could open up if Fiorentina push hard, potentially leaving spaces for Juventus to exploit.
